episode.working_title: Your AI Agents Should Be Running Locally

snapshot.note: Hand-built static snapshot (no automated puller yet). The series below is an explicit cost MODEL derived from the stated assumptions -- it is NOT measured telemetry. Freeze this file; the VO is recorded against these exact numbers and the render must read them unchanged.

topic: Cost of running an always-on AI agent: cloud API vs local hardware

assumptions.workload: 1 always-on coding/agent assistant, heavy multi-step usage ~5M tokens/day (~150M tokens/month)

hero_metrics.label: API fees after breakeven
hero_metrics.context: per-token fees go to $0 -- you own the hardware

flags.breakout_reason: cloud cumulative cost crosses above local total cost of ownership

integrity_notes: The series is a MODEL from the stated assumptions, not measured data. Surface the assumptions on-screen or in the description so we never imply it is observed telemetry.
'100%' refers ONLY to per-token API fees being eliminated, NOT total cost of ownership -- hardware ($2,500) and power (~$40/mo) are real and shown as the flat local line.

Intel Xeon 6+ 'Clearwater Forest' specs VERIFIED 2026-06-01 via multiple tech-press reports (Tom's Hardware, The Register, TechPowerUp): up to 288 cores, Intel 18A process, 576MB L3, launched at Computex on Jun 1 2026. NOTE: it is a data-center chip -- on screen we frame Intel as an industry signal, NOT the consumer rig the viewer runs (avoids implying enterprise hardware is required).
The on-screen Ollama version tag is a placeholder until pinned to the exact installed version.
sources_to_cite: (pending) cloud API pricing page used for the $4/1M blended rate
